title: leontopodium nivale subsp. alpinum
intro: Leontopodium nivale subsp. alpinum, commonly known as Edelweiss, is a small, white-flowering alpine plant native to the European Alps. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of inflammation, pain, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including its potential to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe digestive issues. Edelweiss is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, although its use is largely limited to traditional medicine and is not widely recognized by modern science.
